output.revisionDescription: Removed manual alt text lookup and override, delegated to wp_get_attachment_image() native handling

summary.fixApplied: Removed manual $img_alt variable and 'alt' key override from wp_get_attachment_image()

checks.details: Confirmed: No $img_alt variable declaration found in the file. Previously on line 145-146, now removed.
checks.evidence: Lines 145-153 show wp_get_attachment_image() call with no preceding alt text lookup

checks.details: Confirmed: The attributes array (lines 150-152) only contains 'class' key. No 'alt' key present.
checks.evidence: WordPress's wp_get_attachment_image() will now handle alt text natively from media library metadata

php.improvement: Removed 2 lines of redundant code. WordPress's wp_get_attachment_image() handles alt text automatically from attachment metadata.

tsx.reason: TSX handles editor preview separately from PHP frontend rendering. The fetchMedia() utility provides alt_text from the REST API. Empty string fallback is correct for decorative images.

altTextsFound.alt: Demo landscape photo of campus grounds
Demo nature photo of campus gardens
Demo portrait photo of student activities

altTextVerification.emptyAltHandling: If media library has no alt text set, wp_get_attachment_image() will output alt="" (decorative image)
altTextVerification.comparedToPrevious: Same alt text values as before, but now sourced from WordPress core function instead of manual lookup

checks.details: All images have alt attributes populated from media library
wp_get_attachment_image() automatically includes responsive image attributes
All 10 variations render with correct layout, dark mode, reversing, etc.

changes.before: Manual lookup with empty string fallback
changes.after: WordPress native handling with media library metadata
changes.benefit: More reliable - leverages WordPress core's tested alt text handling. Automatically respects media library settings.

changes.improvement: wp_get_attachment_image() includes all WordPress image attributes: alt, title, srcset, sizes, loading, etc.
changes.complianceLevel: Full WCAG compliance when media library alt text is properly set

accessibilityImpact.wcagCompliance: Meets WCAG 2.1 SC 1.1.1 (Non-text Content) when content editors provide appropriate alt text in media library. Block code correctly delegates to WordPress for alt text handling.

benefits: Removed redundant code - wp_get_attachment_image() already handles alt text
Simplified maintenance - one less thing to keep in sync
Better WordPress integration - uses core function's full capabilities
Automatic responsive images - srcset/sizes included by wp_get_attachment_image()
Future-proof - benefits from WordPress core improvements to image handling
codeQualityImprovement.reasoning: WordPress core functions should be used when available rather than reimplementing their functionality. wp_get_attachment_image() is the recommended way to output attachment images with all proper attributes.

recommendations: Frontend rendering confirmed - all images display with alt text from media library
Code simplified and improved by removing redundant manual alt text lookup
No regressions detected - all block features still work
Block follows WordPress best practices for image output
